stonetop.summary: The proud horse-queen spirit of a wild herd, free and fickle as the wind.

system.concept: The proud horse-queen spirit of a wild herd, free and fickle as the wind.
system.description: A herd of wild horses has a life — nay, a spirit — all its own, a spirit as free and fickle as the wind. Take care, you who would poach wild horses for your own purpose, lest you find your heart turned against you!

questions.prompt: What sort of gift do the ceffylwraig most value?
What sort of gift will do in a pinch?

lore.prompt: **Something interesting:** 'Ceffylwraig' means 'horse wife,' but she sees herself more as queen than wife: proud and haughty, disdainful of the weak and the slow, the herd her subjects more than her children.
**Something useful:** A ceffylwraig is scornful and suspicious of praise or fancy words, but can be appeased by thoughtful gifts, offered with appropriate respect from proud women of noble bearing.
